steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.flere: 'flere' is an infinitive (to weep), not a finite verb
hints.nole: 'nole' is not a real Latin form — 'Noli' is the imperative of 'nolle'
hints.fle: 'fle' is not a standard form — 'Noli' is the finite verb here

steps.target: to be unwilling / to not want
choices: to be unwilling / to not want

hints.to know: 'to know' is 'noscere' — 'Noli' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)
hints.to harm: 'to harm' is 'nocere' — 'Noli' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)
hints.to swim: 'to swim' is 'natare' — 'Noli' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 1st person is 'nolo' — 'Noli' is the imperative singular
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'nolim' — 'Noli' is the imperative for prohibition
hints.infinitive: Infinitive is 'nolle' — 'Noli' is the imperative form

hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are principal parts of 'nosco' (to get to know)
hints.noceo, nocere, nocui, nocitum: These are principal parts of 'noceo' (to harm)
hints.volo, velle, volui, —: These are principal parts of 'volo' (to wish) — 'nolo' is its negative

hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'fle' — 'flere' is the present active infinitive
hints.participle: Participle would be 'flens' — 'flere' is the infinitive
hints.gerund: Gerund would be 'flendi' — 'flere' is the infinitive

hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are principal parts of 'fugio' (to flee)
hints.floreo, florere, florui, —: These are principal parts of 'floreo' (to flourish)
hints.flecto, flectere, flexi, flexum: These are principal parts of 'flecto' (to bend)
